abstract: Abstract Integrating the attention‐based view with the strategic leadership interfaces perspective, we propose a theoretical model of situational urgency mechanisms influencing the allocation of CEOs' attention towards responsive actions. Specifically, we theorize upon the role of humility, which leads CEOs towards embracing interfaces and makes them particularly attentive to the situational context in which they operate. We test our theorization by studying corporate venture capital investments in digital ventures as a response to the situational urgency for digital transformation originating from multiple levels of analysis, internal and external to the firm. Testing a sample of 362 CEOs from 191 firms and 35 industries, we find support for the importance of CEO humility and the moderating role of the lack of top management team digital experience (team‐level urgency) and emerging digital competition (industry‐level urgency). Our study provides important insights into CEO responsiveness and advances our understanding of situated attention through the theorization of situational urgency and its integration with the strategic leadership interfaces perspective.

ob.ai_reason: 涉及数字化转型紧迫感但非AI核心；CEO谦逊与战略领导属OB核心
ob.tldr: 整合注意力基础观与战略领导界面视角，构建情境紧迫感影响CEO注意力分配的模型。研究以362位CEO为样本，考察CEO谦逊特质与数字转型紧迫感如何共同作用于企业风险投资决策。结果支持理论预期：谦逊型CEO更愿意拥抱战略界面；高管团队缺乏数字经验（团队层紧迫感）和新兴数字竞争（行业层紧迫感）均起到正向调节作用，揭示了情境紧迫感如何塑造高管的注意力配置与响应行为。

title: Situated Attention and Strategic Leadership Interfaces: The Role of CEO Humility and Digital Transformation Urgency for Corporate Venture Capital Investments
title_zh: 情境关注与战略领导力接口：CEO谦逊与数字化转型紧迫性对企业风险投资的作用
